incorrects.description: Student selected 4, treating symbol count as the total

steps.text: You counted the symbols. But each symbol stands for more than 1. Check the key. What does each star represent?

incorrects.description: Student selected 9, likely miscounting or using wrong scale

steps.text: Look at the key. Each star equals 5. Count by 5s: one star is 5, two stars is 10. Keep going.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15, stopped counting before the fourth symbol

params.description: All 4 symbols in Swings column highlight.

steps.text: You're counting by 5s. But check how many symbols are in the column. Count all of them: 5, 10, 15... what comes next?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights each symbol in sequence while counting: 5, 10, 15, 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. Look at the key: each star equals 5. Now count: one star is 5, two stars is 10, three stars is 15, four stars is 20. The picture graph shows 20 because we skip count by the scale.
